title: Method Level 1D — Application
summary: Musical phrases and character pieces in C position — apply technique with dynamics, dotted rhythms, and ties inside real melodic contexts. Includes folk songs Jolly Old Saint Nicholas and Yankee Doodle.

exercises.title: Morning Song
exercises.summary: A five-measure right-hand melody with a gentle rise and fall, ending on a dotted half note.
exercises.goal: Shape a musical phrase with clear soft-to-loud contrast and a dotted half ending.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Evening Hymn
exercises.summary: Slow half-note hymn melody descending peacefully to C with a dotted half ending.
exercises.goal: Sustain long tones with a calm, singing quality at piano dynamic.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Dance Tune
exercises.summary: A light six-measure dance in 3/4 with a lift on beat 1.
exercises.goal: Convey a dance character with clear forte dynamic and strong-beat emphasis.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Jolly Old Saint Nicholas
exercises.summary: The beloved Christmas folk song arranged for right hand in C position with dotted half notes.
exercises.goal: Play a recognizable folk melody with dotted half notes and dynamic contrast.

license.attribution: Traditional American Christmas carol, public domain
license.notes: Simplified arrangement in C position for beginner piano.

exercises.title: Walking Song
exercises.summary: A seven-measure walking tune in 2/4 with a steady step feel.
exercises.goal: Play with a light, walking character at forte dynamic.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Bass Morning Song
exercises.summary: Left hand plays the morning song melody in bass clef, ending on a dotted half note.
exercises.goal: Shape a phrase with musical direction in the bass register with dynamic contrast.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Bass Evening Hymn
exercises.summary: Slow hymn melody in bass clef descending to C with a dotted half ending.
exercises.goal: Produce a deep, sustained tone on each half note at piano dynamic.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Bass Dance
exercises.summary: Left hand plays the dance tune in 3/4 bass clef.
exercises.goal: Convey dance character with clear forte emphasis in the bass.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Bass Lullaby
exercises.summary: Gentle left-hand lullaby in bass clef.
exercises.goal: Play softly and smoothly, conveying a rocking lullaby feel at piano dynamic.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Bass Walking Song
exercises.summary: Left hand walks steadily in 2/4 bass clef style.
exercises.goal: Maintain a light, walking character in the bass register at forte.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Morning Duet
exercises.summary: Both hands play the morning song melody together in parallel with dynamic shading.
exercises.goal: Play an identical phrase in both hands with a piano-to-forte swell.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Hymn Together
exercises.summary: Right hand carries the hymn melody with a tied note across the barline while left hand sustains whole-note harmonies.
exercises.goal: Sustain a tied note across a barline while maintaining the left-hand accompaniment.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Dance Together
exercises.summary: Both hands dance in 3/4 — right hand melodic, left hand rhythmic.
exercises.goal: Keep both hands coordinated in a dance character with forte energy.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Lullaby Duet
exercises.summary: Right hand carries the lullaby with a tied C across the barline while left hand rocks gently.
exercises.goal: Sustain a tied note into the final measure for a gentle, held lullaby ending.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Walking Together
exercises.summary: Both hands walk together in 2/4 in parallel motion.
exercises.goal: Sustain a unified walking character with both hands at forte.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Question and Answer — Full
exercises.summary: Right hand asks a forte question; left hand responds piano.
exercises.goal: Shape a call-and-response dialogue with dynamic contrast between hands.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Flowing Contrary
exercises.summary: Hands move in opposite directions with a tied F across the barline then converge on C.
exercises.goal: Track contrary motion with a sustained tied note bridging two measures.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Yankee Doodle
exercises.summary: The patriotic march arranged for both hands with dotted half notes and simple bass accompaniment.
exercises.goal: Play a recognizable march melody with both hands and dotted half note phrasing.

license.attribution: Traditional American patriotic song, public domain
license.notes: Simplified arrangement in C position for beginner piano.

exercises.title: Two Voice Song
exercises.summary: Both hands carry independent melodic lines that move against each other with dynamic shading.
exercises.goal: Maintain two independent voices with clear tone and dynamic contrast.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.

exercises.title: Application Capstone
exercises.summary: A five-measure grand-staff piece with dynamics and a tied ending — the culmination of Level 1D.
exercises.goal: Perform a complete piece with phrase shape, dynamics, and a sustained tied ending.

license.notes: Written for the open starter curriculum.
